records.ety: [F. ingrdient, L. ingrediens, -entis, entering into, p. pr. of ingredi, p. p. ingressus, to go into, to enter; pref. in- in + gradi to walk, go. See Grade.]

defs: That which enters into a compound, or is a component part of any combination, recipe, or mixture; an element; a constituent.
quotes: By way of analysis we may proceed from compounds to ingredients.
Water is the chief ingredient in all the animal fluids and solids.

defs: Entering as, or forming, an ingredient or component part.
quotes: Acts where no sin is ingredient.
